original_abstract: We present the program Top++ for the numerical evaluation of the total inclusive cross-section for producing top quark pairs at hadron colliders. The program calculates the cross-section in a) ﬁxed order approach with exact next-to-next-to leading order (NNLO) accuracy and b) by including soft-gluon resummation for the hadronic cross-section in Mellin space with full next-to-next-to-leading logarithmic (NNLL) accuracy. The program oﬀers the user signiﬁcant ﬂexibility through the large number (29) of available options. Top++ is written in C++. It has a very simple to use interface that is intuitive and directly reﬂects the physics. The running of the program requires no programing experience from the user. Program summary Name of the program: Top++ (ver. 2.0). Program’s homepage: http://www.alexandermitov.com/software License, Warranty: GNU Public License. No warranty given or implied. Compiler: Developed and tested with GNU Compiler Collection’s C++ compiler. Operating system: Linux; Mac OS X; can be adapted for other unix systems. Program language: C++. Memory required to execute: Typically less than 200 MB. External libraries: GNU Scientiﬁc Library (GSL); the Les Houches Accord pdf Interface (LHAPDF).
humanized_abstract: Summary Top++ is a program designed to evaluate the total inclusive cross-section for top quark pair production at the Tevatron and the LHC. In fixed order approach, it works with the full next-to-next-to leading order (NNLO) precision and in the case of including soft-gluon resummation for the hadronic cross-section - with full next-to-next-to-leading logarithmic (NNLL) precision in Mellin space. All options of the program are collected into a section at the beginning of this summary and can be chosen accordingly by the user. In general, the program allows the user to choose between a) fixed order calculation of the cross-section with full next-to-next-to leading order (NNLO) accuracy in the virtual and real corrections, and b) soft-gluon resummation for the hadronic cross-section in Mellin space with full next-to-next-to-leading logarithmic (NNLL) accuracy. Also the program has a large number (29) of other very useful options. Top++ is written entirely in C++. The interface is very simple and easy to use. It is based on common sense that has been preserved as close to the physics as possible. It does not require from the user to know how to program in order to run it. Program name: Top++ Version: 2.0 Program website: http://www.alexandermitov.com/software Top++ is distributed under the GNU Public License. No warranty is given or implied. Compiler: The program has been developed and tested using GNU Compiler Collection’s C++ compiler. Operating system: Linux, Mac OS X. Top++ should also work on other unix systems but it has not been tested. Program language: C++ Memory requirements: The memory required to execute the program is less than 200 MB. External libraries: GSL - The GNU Scientiﬁc Library, LHAPDF - The Les Houches Accord pdf Interface.
